data.title: Graphjet receives Nasdaq letter
data.description:
• Graphjet Technology (NASDAQ:GTI) announced on Friday that it received a Nasdaq notice, indicating that because the company did not regain compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule, requiring that the company maintain a minimum market value of its publicly held shares of $50,000,000 for 30 consecutive trading days, by September 1, 2025, Nasdaq will consider this deficiency in their decision regarding the company’s continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • The Notice has no immediate effect on the listing of the company’s ordinary shares on Nasdaq, which will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “GTI”.
  • However, Nasdaq has requested the company to present its views with respect to this notice in writing no later than September 9, 2025.
  • The company has previously agreed with Nasdaq that it would provide Nasdaq with an update regarding the company’s fundraising plans on or before September 30, 2025.
  • GTI -1.76% after hours to $3.34.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Boeing secures $343.99M U.S. Army contract modification
data.description:
• Boeing, Mesa, Arizona, was awarded a $343.99M modification to a contract to exercise options for remanufactured aircraft and associated support. 
  • The modification brings the total cumulative face value of the contract to $3.43 billion.
  • Work locations and funding will be determined with each order, with an estimated completion date of Dec. 31, 2027.
  • Army Contracting Command, Redstone Arsenal, Alabama, is the contracting activity.

data.title: Rapport Therapeutics soars 23% on release of phase 2 data for seizure asset
data.description:
• Rapport Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RAPP) surged 23% in after-hours trading Friday after announcing that it would release top-line phase 2a data on RAP-219, its candidate drug-resistant focal onset seizures.
  • The company is also expected to release a primary endpoint analysis of the small molecule drug examining the proportion of patients achieving ≥30% reduction in long-episodes compared with 12-week baseline period as well as median percent change in long episode frequency compared with 12-week baseline period. Long episodes are considered a biomarker of clinical seizures.
  • Rapport will hold a conference call and webcast on Sept. 8 at 8a ET.

data.title: Gold rises to another record as U.S. jobs data strengthens case for rate cuts
data.description:
Gold futures jumped Friday, with the front-month contract closing above $3,600/oz for the first time, after the latest disappointing U.S. jobs data likely sealed the case for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates at its next meeting later this month.

The U.S. added just 22K jobs in August, below the 75K gain economists had expected, while the unemployment rate ticked higher to 4.3%, in line with expectations, and June's employment data was revised lower to show a contraction of 13K jobs, while July's figure was revised slightly higher to a gain of 79K jobs, the Labor Department reported.

Investors are now fully pricing in a quarter-point rate cut at the Fed's September 16-17 policy meeting, and are moving closer to anticipating a total of three rate cuts this year, according to futures contracts; non-interest bearing gold tends to perform well when interest rates are low and uncertainty is high.

Analysts also have noted the independence of the Fed as a key factor behind gold's recent gains following President Trump's efforts to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook, after repeatedly pressuring the central bank to cut rates.

The recent surge in the gold price - up 7% during a run of seven gains in eight sessions - followed President Trump's move to oust Cook and replace her with a rate cut supporter.

Riding the rally in the metal, the NYSE Arca Gold Miners Index hit an all-time high Friday, as some of the largest stocks in the benchmark have soared, including 80%-plus YTD gains for Newmont (NEM), Agnico Eagle Mines (AEM), Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM), and Barrick Mining (B).

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for September delivery closed +1.3% to $3,613.20/oz, another record high settlement, while front-month Comex September silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.4% to $41.074/oz, the metal's sixth gain in seven sessions and second-highest settlement this year.

For the week, gold gained for five of the past six weeks, jumping 4% in its best percentage gain since May, and silver added 2.2% for the week.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: TLGY Acquisition, SC Assets add $530M PIPE, form strategic advisory board
data.description:
• TLGY Acquisition (OTC:TLGYF) and StablecoinX Assets announced an additional $530 million in PIPE financing, bringing total committed PIPE proceeds to $890 million in connection with their proposed business combination.

  • The financing follows the $360 million PIPE announced in July.
  • The financing will support StablecoinX’s multi-year treasury strategy focused on accumulating ENA, the native token of the Ethena ecosystem.
  • At closing, the combined company is now expected to hold over 3 billion ENA tokens at closing of the transaction. 
  • The Ethena Foundation will launch a $310 million follow-on token buyback program, building on a previous $260 million buyback, totaling $570 million to date.
  • Company said, the additional PIPE financing includes participation from YZi Labs, Brevan Howard, Susquehanna Crypto, and IMC Trading, as well as investors who participated in the initial PIPE, including Dragonfly, ParaFi Capital, Maven11, Kingsway, Mirana, and Haun Ventures.
  • TLGYF closed +0.54% at $13.02.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BioVaxys announces share consolidation
data.description:
• BioVaxys Technology (OTCQB:BVAXF) on Friday announced the consolidation of its common shares on the basis of ten pre-consolidation common shares for one post-consolidation common share.
  • The company anticipates its common shares will commence trading on a post-consolidation basis on the Canadian Securities Exchange at market open on September 11, 2025.
  • The company currently has 293.42M common shares issued and outstanding. Following the consolidation, the company expects to have 29.34M common shares issued and outstanding.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chijet Motor Company announces issuance of ordinary shares to holders of contingent value rights
data.description:
• Chijet Motor Company (NASDAQ:CJET) on Friday said it has issued a total of 640,850 class A ordinary shares of par value $0.003 to the holders of contingent value rights on record as of July 22, 2025 pro rata, based on their respective number of CVRs held.
  • The record date for the Qualifying CVR Holders to receive such shares was July 22, 2025.
  • CJET -1.28% after hours to $0.1542.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DIH Holding US receives Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• DIH Holdings (NASDAQ:DHAI) on Friday said it has received a delisting notice from Nasdaq after failing to meet the $50 million market value of listed securities (MVLS) requirement by the September 1, 2025 deadline.
  • The company had been under a 180-day compliance period since March 5.
  • DIH intends to request a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel, which would temporarily halt delisting proceedings.
  • The company will seek an extension and present a plan to regain compliance.
  • No assurance was given that the extension will be granted or that compliance will be achieved.
  • DHAI +0.89% after hours to $0.247.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Anthropic settles to pay $1.5B to authors in landmark copyright case over AI training
data.description:
Anthropic has settled a civil suit and agreed to pay authors $1.5B in a landmark copyright case involving the use of training artificial intelligence models on pirated copies of books.

Anthropic, which is backed by Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and other tech giants, was accused by several authors of downloading pirated copies of books to train its Claude chatbot. According to details of the settlement, Anthropic will pay $3,000 for each of the approximately 500,000 pirated books used to train Claude. It will also destroy two of its datasets. 

The $1.5B settlement deal must still be approved by U.S. District Judge William Alsup for the Northern District of California in San Francisco. A hearing for the settlement has been set for Monday, Sept. 8. 

"The proposed settlement well surpasses other copyright recoveries, will provide meaningful compensation for each Class Work, and will set a precedent of AI companies paying for their use of pirated websites like Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror," reads the motion for the settlement, which was filed today.

The case, which is filed under Bartz et al v. Anthropic PBC, 24-cv-05417, began last year when authors Andrea Bartz, Charles Graeber and Kirk Wallace Johnson sued the startup. A larger group of writers and publishers eventually joined the suit. 

Alsup issued a split decision in his summary judgment order earlier this summer. He determined that training AI models on purchased, copyrighted books that were digitized was fair use. However, training models on copyrighted books obtained through pirate websites is not.

"The Court reached different conclusions regarding different sources of training data," according to court documents. "It found that reproducing purchased and scanned books to train AI constituted fair use. However, the Court denied summary judgment on the copyright infringement claims related to the works Anthropic obtained from Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror."

"The proposed settlement well surpasses other copyright recoveries, will provide meaningful compensation for each Class Work, and will set a precedent of AI companies paying for their use of pirated websites like Library Genesis and Pirate Library Mirror," according to the settlement. 

The settlement sends a "strong message to the AI industry that there are serious consequences when they pirate authors' works to train their AI, robbing those least able to afford it," said Authors Guild CEO Mary Rasenberger.

If Anthropic had not agreed to a settlement, the case was expected to go to trial this December, which could have eventually cost Anthropic even more. Anthropic faces another civil suit by Reddit (RDDT) for allegedly using the social media company's data without a licensing agreement.

Other AI companies are facing similar suits. OpenAI and its primary backer, Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), have been sued by the New York Times (NYT) for alleged copyright infringement for using the newspaper's content to train AI models.

In June, a federal judge sided with Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) in dismissing a copyright infringement lawsuit of authors who alleged the company stole their works to train its AI models.

data.title: DBV Technologies launches $150M ATM offering
data.description:
• DBV Technologies (NASDAQ:DBVT) filed a prospectus supplement with the SEC for an at-the-market offering up to $150M of American Depositary Shares.
  • Each ADS represents five ordinary shares of the company.
  • The new ATM Program replaces the prior ATM program from May 2022, with similar terms and conditions.
  • Proceeds will primarily fund the Biologics License Application and potential launch of the VIASKIN Peanut patch for toddlers aged 1-3 years.
  • Additional funds will support ongoing development of the VIASKIN technology platform, working capital, and general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press release
